notes: Run this benchmark from the repository root.
The scenario exercises the backlog-capacity refusal added in Tiber 0.11.0.
The verifier checks that the skill preserves the explicit replace/combine/reject decision without retrying or inventing hidden work.

scenarios.title: Handle a full configured backlog
scenarios.purpose: Check that new-task treats capacity refusal as a user admission decision rather than a retryable creation failure.
scenarios.userInput: Use the local Codex skill "new-task". In this copied workspace, inspect plugins/tiber/skills/new-task/SKILL.md and write .plugin-eval-benchmark/new-task.md with the exact response to `tiber.backlog_capacity_exceeded queued=5 max_queued=5`. The response must state whether a task was created, refuse automatic retry or any overflow/icebox/shadow/hidden backlog, and present the three explicit choices: replace a lower-value queued task, combine genuinely overlapping work, or reject the candidate.
successChecklist: The artifact says no task was created.
It does not retry or retain hidden work.
It presents replace, combine, and reject as the explicit admission outcomes.
